page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the N2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The instance features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs, mapped from 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place the performance of this instance consistently in the average tier. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test yields a score of 30011.12, while the single-core performance reaches 1837.79. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1654.01 GB/sec, which drops to 166.42 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 85.05 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance achieves a PassMark database score of 11079.07 and processes 26.98 million Redis SET operations per second. Software compilation with Clang scores 21814.0 on Geekbench, and static web serving throughput reaches 21.35 GB/sec.

The instance supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions, which assist in cryptographic and integer operations. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware limits its utility for high-speed local database caching or heavy parallel computing. While it handles small to medium language model inference adequately, processing 41.93 prompt tokens per second on a 7B model, its performance on large 70B models is poor at 4.16 tokens per second. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for balanced workloads such as enterprise web serving, software compilation, medium-scale database operations, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-32 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, utilizing Intel Xeon processors running at 2.8 GHz. It features 16 physical cores with hyper-threading and provides 4.0 GB of memory per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 30011.12 and a PassMark CPU score of 27193.8. It demonstrates solid capabilities for database operations, processing 26.98 million Redis SET operations per second, and achieves a static web serving throughput of 21.35 GB/sec. While it supports AVX-512 instructions, its lack of local disks and poor large-scale LLM inference performance represent clear tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, software compilation, and standard web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-32 is a general-purpose instance with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM. It offers balanced performance for enterprise web serving and database workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-32 is a general-purpose VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a one-to-four virtual CPU to memory ratio.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory.
Balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose workloads.
Supports AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions.
Average multi-core performance with 30011.12 stress-ng score.
Best suited for enterprise web serving, software compilation, and database hosting.
